title: Team Norms: Code Review Expectations
bodyMarkdown:
How the Helios team reviews code. Written down so expectations do not depend on who happens to review your change.

## Turnaround
First review pass within one business day. If you are swamped, say so in the channel rather than letting a review sit silent, someone else will pick it up.

## What reviewers check
Correctness first, then whether the change matches existing idioms in the touched module. A change to src/checkpoint should read like the rest of src/checkpoint. Style nits are fine but should not block a merge on their own; leave them as non-blocking comments.

## Who reviews what
Priya Natarajan reviews storage and checkpoint changes. Owen Reyes reviews serving and the API surface. Elena Petrova reviews anything touching metrics, deploy tooling, or on-call runbooks. Jonah Kim reviews compiler changes. Cross-cutting changes need one reviewer from each affected area.

## Small PRs
Prefer several small, reviewable changes over one large one. A PR that touches five modules to fix one bug is a sign the fix should be split, or that the bug is bigger than it looked.

## Disagreements
If reviewer and author disagree after one round of discussion, loop in Maya Okafor rather than let a PR stall indefinitely.
